notes: CRITICAL — all clear. JS only (no TS files added). No asset loading (pure Phaser Graphics calls). Deterministic: SEED = 0x5eed matches terrain seed at line 485; XOR sub-seed 0xABCD1234 used for spawn rolls to avoid aliasing. LANDMARK_CAP = 80 enforced as double-loop exit condition. Spawn probabilities confirmed: desert 0.08, grassland 0.04, mountain 0.06, rock 0.05, urban 0.02, water 0, unknown 0. No terrain/path/node/click handler lines touched (confirmed scope attribution per orchestrator instruction). HIGH — all clear. Per-biome shapes match spec: DESERT 70/30 cactus (3x8 trunk + arms) / rock_formation (3 filled triangles), GRASSLAND scrub (3 circles), MOUNTAIN ridge_line (2-3 lineBetween calls), ROCK boulder (Phaser slice semicircle), URBAN structure (strokeRect + door fillRect). Shadow: fillStyle(0x000000, 0.15) + fillEllipse(lx, ly+1, 6, 2) — 1px ellipse offset 1px down, alpha 0.15, matches spec exactly. Layer order correct per orchestrator resolution: landmark container created at default depth 0, nodes created after in same default-depth pool, so creation order guarantees nodes render on top of landmarks. Call site in create() is after _renderPathLayer and before node-markers loop (lines 174-182 confirmed). MEDIUM — performance.now() bracket present at lines 631 and 787; console.debug log at lines 790-795 includes per-biome breakdown (desert/grassland/mountain/rock/urban counts) and seed confirmation. Receipt accuracy: files_changed lists only WorldMapScene.js, consistent with diff scope. No exports_added declared; _renderLandmarkLayer is a private method, correct. LOW — defensive setDepth missing on _landmarkContainer (defaults to 0, same as node circle/text objects). Visually correct today because _renderLandmarkLayer runs before the node loop. Fragile: any future agent that calls explicit setDepth(0) on another object could collide. Recommend _landmarkContainer.setDepth(-25) in a follow-up cycle. Not blocking. Minor: the bounds-check guard at line 693 is logically redundant (cx is computed from mapLeft + tx*TILE + TILE/2, so it can never be < mapLeft or > mapLeft+mapW for valid tx in [0,cols)), but harmless dead-code; not a FAIL.
